questions.question: Compare the four main categories of clustering algorithms with examples.
keyPoints: Partitional: K-Means, requires K, spherical clusters
Hierarchical: Agglomerative/Divisive, dendrograms, no K needed
Density-based: DBSCAN, arbitrary shapes, outlier detection
Grid-based: STING, CLIQUE, fast on large data
Each suited for different data characteristics

questions.question: Explain the Silhouette Score and its interpretation.
keyPoints: Used to evaluate clustering quality and choose K

questions.question: When would you choose DBSCAN over K-Means?
keyPoints: When number of clusters is unknown

questions.question: Describe the Elbow Method for determining the optimal number of clusters.
keyPoints: Run K-Means for different K values
Plot WCSS (within-cluster sum of squares) vs K
Identify the elbow point where curve bends
Beyond elbow, diminishing returns from more clusters
